name: Atopic Dermatitis - Comprehensive Provider Query (All Paths)
description: Simulates an experienced dermatologist who knows that atopic dermatitis patients may not always have a formal diagnosis code in the EMR. Some patients are managed entirely by primary care with topical corticosteroid prescriptions and antihistamines but never receive a formal dermatology diagnosis code. The prompt asks the LLM to search across both diagnosis codes and medication records to identify the complete AD population. This tests clinical reasoning about skin conditions where treatment often precedes formal diagnosis.
prompt: Find all patients with atopic dermatitis. Search both diagnoses and AD-related medication prescriptions to capture the complete population.

prompts.naive: Find all patients with atopic dermatitis. Search both diagnoses and AD-related medication prescriptions to capture the complete population.
prompts.expert:
I need to identify my complete atopic dermatitis patient population for a clinical research study. I know from experience that not all eczema patients have a formal diagnosis code in their chart. Many patients are managed by their primary care physician with over-the-counter hydrocortisone cream and oral antihistamines like cetirizine or diphenhydramine, and the atopic dermatitis diagnosis was never formally documented in the problem list. Some patients only show up through their prescription records for topical steroids or prednisone tapers for severe flares. Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:

1. Patients with an atopic dermatitis or eczema diagnosis on their problem list
2. Patients who have been prescribed medications commonly used for atopic dermatitis management, including topical corticosteroids (hydrocortisone cream), antihistamines (cetirizine, diphenhydramine), and oral corticosteroids (prednisone) for flare management

For each query, return the matching resources. I want to see the union of all patients found across both approaches.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Comprehensive multi-query approach that mirrors how a real dermatologist would identify the full atopic dermatitis population. Requires the LLM to generate 2 separate FHIR queries and combine results. Tests clinical reasoning about dermatologic conditions where topical treatment often precedes formal diagnosis.

expected_query.notes: This test case expects MULTIPLE queries (see metadata.expected_queries). The expected_query.url is the minimum viable query. Evaluation unions patient IDs from both queries to get the complete atopic dermatitis population.
